name: Dinton
overview: Dinton is a pretty village in the Nadder Valley about 8 miles west of Salisbury and around 5 miles from Wilton, on the chalk downs dividing the Nadder and Wylye valleys. The B3089 runs through its centre, and the National Trust's Dinton Park and the Neo-Grecian Philipps House (plus Little Clarendon, Hyde's House and Lawes Cottage) sit on its edge. The village absorbed the neighbouring parish of Baverstock in 1934 and lies partly within the Cranborne Chase National Landscape.
character: A thriving, history-rich downland village with two pubs, a primary school and extensive National Trust parkland, retaining a strong community feel.
amenities: Two pubs: The Wyndham Arms and The Penruddocke Arms
Village hall with pre-school and many clubs
Large recreation ground with football and cricket pitches and playgrounds
National Trust Dinton Park and Philipps House (230 acres)
Annual Dinton Fireworks and Fete at Philipps House
Bus services to Wilton and Salisbury

commutes.notes: Via Tisbury station (West of England line), a short drive away

prices.summary: A desirable Nadder Valley village; local-stone period cottages and family homes near National Trust parkland, with values supported by the school and Cranborne Chase setting. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walks in National Trust Dinton Park (views to Salisbury Cathedral spire)
Visiting Philipps House and Little Clarendon
The Monarch's Way footpath and Grovely Wood
Two Mills on the Nadder walk
placesToEat: The Penruddocke Arms (free house with Sunday carvery)
pros: Good-rated primary school and lively village hall
National Trust parkland and Philipps House on the doorstep
Tisbury mainline station a short drive away
cons: No railway station in the village (Tisbury is nearest)
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a characterful Nadder Valley village with pubs, a good school and National Trust parkland, within reach of Salisbury and Tisbury rail.

sources.label: Rural View - Dinton village and primary school
